name: Scott LaRocca
focus: Tropical forests • Ecohydrology • Microwave remote sensing
bio: Scott LaRocca is a PhD student in Ecological and Environmental Informatics at Northern Arizona University. His research focuses on the ecohydrology and ecophysiology of tropical forests under severe drought, using remote sensing approaches including microwave and thermal data to track changes in canopy water content and forest function. He is broadly interested in advancing technologies for monitoring of terrestrial ecosystems, including the use of UAVs, in situ sensor networks, and machine learning. Scott is passionate about field-based science and connecting Earth system research with communities and conservation outcomes.
